authorization_phrase_detected: YES start R2 new POWER_LOTTO strategy discovery from feature engineering, read-only planning first, no DB write

key_findings.p161: Zero strategies beat random baseline after Bonferroni/BH correction (family=40). Pool mean +0.020 above random (0.9674 vs 0.9474), no corrected significance. POWER_LOTTO at-random NULL confirmed.
key_findings.p167: 6-module ensemble/voting research: Module F final gate FAILED. Ensemble did not consistently beat random or best-single-strategy across OOS windows. No defensible edge from combining existing strategies.
key_findings.p170: 5 sensitivity threshold scenarios (S1-S5), 2 held-out signal tracking targets. Signal non-stationary confirmed. Window 2 below random at all thresholds (S1-S3 means 0.929-0.938 vs baseline 0.9474). Held-out signals A=0.865, E=0.920, both below random. P167 NULL result strengthened.

existing_strategy_names: 威力彩 冷號互補 2注
r1_summary.conclusion: R1 existing-strategy ensemble/voting research closed. No defensible edge found in existing strategy space. Pivoting to R2 new strategy discovery.

reason_for_pivoting_to_r2: User explicitly requested exploring approaches that are not just recombinations of existing strategies. R1 has exhausted the signal space defined by Fourier, Markov, MidFreq, Orthogonal, FreqOrt, PP3, Precision, Zonal Entropy, and Cold Number signals. R2 starts fresh from feature engineering to generate genuinely novel strategy hypotheses that have not been evaluated in R1.

feature_engineering_inventory.data_source_note: F04 and F08 require lottery_draws table (historical draw records, not replay DB). F01-F03, F05-F07, F09-F10 can use replay DB columns only. All features must be computable from pre-target draws only to avoid data leakage.

families.name: recency_frequency_features
families.description: Exponentially decay-weighted number hit frequency. Recent draws weighted more heavily than distant history. Window sizes: 20, 50, 100, 200 draws.
families.leakage_risk: LOW — decay weights applied to draws strictly before target_draw
families.notes: Distinct from MidFreq (uniform window count). Decay factor is a free hyperparameter — must be pre-declared before OOS evaluation.

families.name: rolling_hot_cold_features
families.description: Rolling windows of above-median vs below-median hit frequency. Hot (>75th pct), Cold (<25th pct), Neutral designation per number per window. Hot-cold transition count.
families.leakage_risk: LOW — rolling window anchored strictly before target_draw
families.notes: More granular than 冷號互補 (raw absence count). Adds transition counting. Threshold percentiles must be pre-declared.

families.name: positional_frequency_and_number_position_interaction
families.description: Per-bet-position (slot) frequency of each number value. Number-slot correlation matrix. Slot-specific entropy as diversity measure.
families.leakage_risk: MEDIUM — must aggregate slots strictly before target_draw; full-history aggregation is leakage
families.notes: Distinct from FreqOrt (orthogonality constraint). Uses actual slot co-placement patterns. Requires bet_index column to be present.

families.name: pair_triplet_co_occurrence_features
families.description: Number co-occurrence frequencies for pairs and triplets within the same draw. Graph-based degree centrality. Pair co-occurrence rate normalized by pool size.
families.leakage_risk: LOW if using only pre-target draws from historical lottery records
families.notes: Requires external lottery_draws data, not replay DB rows. None of the 10 existing strategies use co-occurrence graph structure. Pair space C(38,2)=703 edges — Bonferroni correction mandatory.

families.name: gap_overdue_last_seen_distance_features
families.description: Number of draws since each number last appeared (gap). Running overdue score. Standardized gap vs expected gap under fair geometric distribution.
families.leakage_risk: LOW — last-seen defined strictly before target_draw
families.notes: Distinct from 冷號互補 — adds z-score normalization against theoretical geometric distribution (mean gap = 38/6 ≈ 6.33 for POWER_LOTTO main). Provides principled overdue measure.

families.name: parity_odd_even_balance_features
families.description: Proportion of odd vs even numbers in each draw. Odd-even streak length. Deviation from expected 50/50 balance under fair random model.
families.notes: POWER_LOTTO 1-38 has 19 odd / 19 even — expected ratio exactly 0.5. None of the 10 existing strategies explicitly model parity balance.

families.name: high_low_zone_distribution_features
families.description: Partition 1-38 into low (1-13), mid (14-25), high (26-38) zones. Zone count distribution per draw. Zone frequency imbalance detection. Historical zone-count distribution as target.
families.notes: Existing Zonal Entropy uses entropy of prediction set only. This family adds explicit zone-count features and cross-zone frequency modeling. Zone boundaries are fixed — no boundary-tuning leakage.

families.name: sum_span_variance_dispersion_features
families.description: Sum of 6 main numbers per draw. Span (max-min). Variance. Mean absolute deviation. Rolling percentile of these statistics against historical distribution.
families.leakage_risk: LOW if distribution parameters computed from pre-target history only
families.notes: Characterizes draw-level diversity independent of individual number frequencies. No existing strategy explicitly models draw composition dispersion.

families.name: modulo_residue_class_features
families.description: Numbers classified by mod-3, mod-5, mod-7 residue classes. Residue class frequency imbalance. Cross-mod interaction counts per draw.
families.notes: Genuinely novel for POWER_LOTTO. L84 showed ACB boundary/mod3 heuristic failed for BIG_LOTTO, but this is a pure distributional residue feature, not a hard inclusion rule. Different mechanism.

families.name: trend_shift_regime_window_features
families.description: CUSUM change-point detection on hit rates. Regime label (high/mid/low activity) computed over rolling windows of 50/100/200 draws. Regime transition probability and stability score.
families.leakage_risk: MEDIUM — CUSUM must use one-sided historical-only computation; two-sided CUSUM is leakage
families.notes: More principled than existing Regime strategy which uses retrospective labeling. P170 confirmed non-stationarity — adaptive regime detection is the correct response. CUSUM threshold must be pre-declared.

strategy_candidates.name: weighted_recency_frequency_strategy
strategy_candidates.hypothesis: Numbers that have appeared more frequently in recent draws under exponential decay weighting have a marginally higher probability of appearing in future draws. Recency weighting reduces noise from distant historical draws in a non-stationary environment.
strategy_candidates.expected_output_format: Ranked number list (1-38 for main); top-6 selected per draw
strategy_candidates.overfitting_risk: MEDIUM — decay rate hyperparameter must be pre-declared; no sweep after seeing OOS
strategy_candidates.validation_method: Walk-forward OOS (min 200 draws); permutation test vs 36/38 random baseline; Bonferroni over decay rate grid
strategy_candidates.reason_not_recombining_old_strategies: Existing MidFreq uses uniform window count. Exponential decay weighting is a fundamentally different signal transformation with different theoretical motivation (non-stationarity hypothesis confirmed by P170).

strategy_candidates.name: gap_adjusted_overdue_strategy
strategy_candidates.hypothesis: Numbers absent significantly longer than their expected geometric inter-arrival time under a fair random model carry a measurable overdue signal. Selecting statistically overdue numbers improves hit rate vs unconstrained random selection.
strategy_candidates.expected_output_format: Numbers ranked by overdue z-score; top-6 selected per draw
strategy_candidates.overfitting_risk: LOW — single signal with one tunable threshold parameter
strategy_candidates.validation_method: Walk-forward OOS (min 200 draws); compare against 冷號互補 baseline; permutation test
strategy_candidates.reason_not_recombining_old_strategies: 冷號互補 uses raw absence count. This strategy normalizes by theoretical geometric distribution (mean gap = 38/6 ≈ 6.33), making the overdue measure statistically grounded rather than count-based.

strategy_candidates.name: co_occurrence_pair_graph_strategy
strategy_candidates.hypothesis: Certain number pairs co-occur more frequently than chance in historical draws. A graph-based centrality measure identifies numbers participating in many high-frequency pairs, suggesting structural non-randomness in number combinations.
strategy_candidates.expected_output_format: Numbers ranked by co-occurrence centrality score; top-6 selected per draw
strategy_candidates.leakage_risk: LOW — graph built from pre-target draws only
strategy_candidates.overfitting_risk: HIGH — pair space C(38,2)=703 edges; graph construction window must be pre-declared; Bonferroni correction over pair count mandatory
strategy_candidates.validation_method: Walk-forward OOS (min 300 draws); Bonferroni over pair count; permutation test against pair-random null; compare against P161 best strategy
strategy_candidates.reason_not_recombining_old_strategies: None of the 10 existing strategies use co-occurrence graph structure. This is a fundamentally different representation of lottery history — adjacency relationships rather than individual number frequencies.

strategy_candidates.name: zone_balanced_frequency_strategy
strategy_candidates.hypothesis: Historical draws exhibit characteristic zone distributions. Predictions constrained to match historical zone balance (low/mid/high proportions) and ranked by zone-adjusted frequency outperform unconstrained frequency selection.
strategy_candidates.expected_output_format: Candidate sets filtered to satisfy zone balance constraints; frequency-ranked within constraints; top-6 selected
strategy_candidates.overfitting_risk: MEDIUM — zone boundaries fixed (1-13, 14-25, 26-38); zone-count targets must be pre-declared from training distribution
strategy_candidates.validation_method: Walk-forward OOS (min 200 draws); compare against unconstrained frequency baseline; permutation test
strategy_candidates.reason_not_recombining_old_strategies: Existing Zonal Entropy measures entropy of prediction set. This strategy explicitly constrains selection to match the empirical zone count distribution of historical draws — a different constraint mechanism.

strategy_candidates.name: entropy_dispersion_controlled_strategy
strategy_candidates.hypothesis: Predictions whose sum and span statistics match the historical draw distribution cover more of the number space and avoid systematic biases. Controlling dispersion toward the historical distribution improves coverage and hit rate.
strategy_candidates.expected_output_format: Candidate sets scored by dispersion-match to historical distribution; top-6 by composite score
strategy_candidates.leakage_risk: LOW if historical distribution parameters computed from pre-target draws only
strategy_candidates.overfitting_risk: MEDIUM — dispersion target parameters must be pre-declared from training data
strategy_candidates.validation_method: Walk-forward OOS (min 200 draws); permutation test; compare against sum-matched random baseline (C08)
strategy_candidates.reason_not_recombining_old_strategies: No existing strategy explicitly models draw-level dispersion (sum, span, MAD). This is a draw-composition feature rather than a number-frequency feature — a different level of abstraction.

strategy_candidates.name: regime_adaptive_window_strategy
strategy_candidates.hypothesis: POWER_LOTTO hit rates are non-stationary (P170 confirmed Window 2 degradation at all thresholds). A strategy that detects regime shifts via CUSUM and adapts its selection window to the current regime outperforms fixed-window strategies in a non-stationary environment.
strategy_candidates.expected_output_format: Regime-labeled frequency ranking; window size adapts per detected regime label; top-6 selected
strategy_candidates.leakage_risk: MEDIUM — CUSUM must use one-sided historical-only lookback; future draws must never enter regime detection
strategy_candidates.overfitting_risk: HIGH — CUSUM threshold and window size per regime must be pre-declared; no tuning on OOS data
strategy_candidates.validation_method: Walk-forward OOS (min 300 draws); compare against fixed-window P161 best strategy; compare against P167 ensemble; permutation test
strategy_candidates.reason_not_recombining_old_strategies: Existing Regime strategy uses retrospective labeling — it knows the regime in hindsight. CUSUM-based online regime detection is a principled change-point method that operates causally, without future information.

strategy_candidates.name: hybrid_rank_aggregation_strategy
strategy_candidates.hypothesis: Aggregating independent feature-based rankings (recency, gap, zone, parity) via Borda count produces a more robust signal than any single feature alone. Rank fusion is more stable than signal fusion when individual signals are noisy.
strategy_candidates.expected_output_format: Borda-count aggregated rank; top-6 selected per draw
strategy_candidates.leakage_risk: LOW — all component features are pre-target only
strategy_candidates.overfitting_risk: HIGH — feature weights must be pre-declared; no weight tuning after seeing OOS; Bonferroni over weight grid size
strategy_candidates.validation_method: Walk-forward OOS (min 300 draws); compare against each component individually; compare against P167 ensemble; permutation test; Bonferroni over weight grid
strategy_candidates.reason_not_recombining_old_strategies: P167 ensemble combines existing strategy prediction outputs (number sets). This candidate aggregates raw feature rankings (inputs), a different level of fusion. Features F01, F05, F06, F07 are not extracted by any existing strategy.

strategy_candidates.name: constrained_random_baseline_challenger
strategy_candidates.hypothesis: A purely random baseline constrained only by zone balance (F07) and parity (F06) provides a tighter null model than unconstrained random. If a new strategy cannot beat this constrained random baseline, it has no defensible edge beyond structural constraints.
strategy_candidates.expected_output_format: Random draw constrained to historical zone count and parity distribution; used as comparison baseline only — not as a predictive strategy
strategy_candidates.overfitting_risk: NOT_APPLICABLE — null model only, not a predictive strategy
strategy_candidates.validation_method: Used as secondary comparison baseline in all R2 validation runs. Not itself subject to significance testing as a predictive strategy.
strategy_candidates.reason_not_recombining_old_strategies: R1 compared against fully unconstrained random (36/38 baseline). This provides a tighter null model accounting for known structural constraints. Required to prevent overclaiming edge against a weaker baseline.

allowed_in_p172: plan-only or minimal feature extraction prototype implementation
pre-declared strategy configs stored in plan artifacts only
OOS evaluation scripts that do not modify DB or registry

r2_validation_protocol.principle: No strategy passes unless it demonstrates reproducible OOS superiority over random and existing strategies under pre-registered configuration.

steps.name: time_ordered_train_test_split
steps.description: All splits strictly time-ordered by target_draw. No shuffling. Train = earliest draws, test = latest draws. Test set must never be used during feature or hyperparameter selection.

steps.name: walk_forward_oos_evaluation
steps.description: Walk-forward expanding window. Start with minimum 500 training draws. Evaluate on rolling 50-draw blocks. Accumulate OOS results without any refitting on OOS data.

steps.name: pre_declared_strategy_configs
steps.description: All strategy hyperparameters declared in the P172 plan artifact before any OOS evaluation. No parameter tuning after seeing OOS results. Treated as a pre-registration requirement.

steps.name: random_baseline_comparison
steps.description: Primary: 36/38 fair-random main number baseline (P161 confirmed). Secondary: constrained random baseline (C08) with zone and parity constraints.

steps.name: p161_best_strategy_comparison
steps.description: Must outperform P161 best single strategy (midfreq_fourier_mk_3bet, raw hit rate 0.9674) in OOS. Not just in training. OOS comparison is the only valid gate.

steps.name: p167_ensemble_comparison
steps.description: Must outperform P167 ensemble best OOS window result. P167 showed no consistent OOS edge — new strategy must show consistent superiority across OOS windows.

steps.name: multiple_testing_correction
steps.description: Bonferroni correction over number of candidate strategies tested. BH correction as secondary. Family size = number of candidates × number of hyperparameter configurations. Report both corrected and uncorrected p-values.

steps.name: honest_null_reporting
steps.description: If no candidate beats the corrected significance threshold: report NULL honestly. Do not claim edge based on uncorrected p-values. Do not retroactively reframe null results as positive signals. R2 NULL is acceptable and must be reported as such.

next_task_authorization_required_phrase: YES start P172 POWER_LOTTO new strategy prototype plan
